response.justification: The candidate brings genuine geospatial domain experience (GISCollective, Green Map, FOSS4G talk) and has built low-latency, high-throughput services in DLang with async I/O and fibers, which is adjacent to the systems work this role demands. However, the core requirement is modern C++ (or Rust), and his only professional C++ exposure was a roughly seven-month stint at National Instruments ending in 2015; his recent work at Google and Meta is TypeScript/Node full-stack and product-oriented. He has no routing, tiling, or navigation-pipeline experience, so despite strong engineering craftsmanship and testing culture, the fit for a C++-centric routing data pipeline role is uncertain.
strengths: Real geospatial/GIS experience: founded an open-source geo-data platform used by Green Map and spoke at FOSS4G
Low-latency systems work at Sociomantic handling millions of requests per second using async I/O, fibers, and caching in DLang
Strong quality culture: TDD, CI/CD, authored test frameworks (Fluent-Asserts, Trial), and taught testing workshops
concerns: Modern C++ experience is minimal and dated (~7 months at National Instruments in 2014-2015); no Rust
No experience with routing engines, map tiling, or navigation guidance systems specifically
Recent trajectory (Google, Meta, RIDE) is TypeScript/Node full-stack product work, far from systems-level pipeline engineering
